questions_zh: 不用看原文，解释 feature、circuit、intervention 三个词分别是什么意思，以及它们之间是什么关系。
为什么 Zoom In 这套视觉电路故事对初学者非常有用，但直接照搬到语言模型上会出问题？
如果把 M00 当成后续 interpretability 研究的起点，最先会被哪一个现象打破：单语义神经元、局部电路可视化，还是因果解释？为什么？
questions_en: Without looking back at the note, explain what feature, circuit, and intervention mean and how they relate to one another.
Why is the Zoom In visual-circuits story so useful for beginners, yet risky to transfer directly to language models?
If M00 is the starting picture for later interpretability work, which part breaks first: monosemantic neurons, directly visible local circuits, or causal explanation? Why?

questions_zh: 为什么 superposition 更像一个几何结果，而不只是训练中的 bug？
在你的 toy model sweep 里，hidden dimension、稀疏度或 loss 项的变化，哪一个最明显地改变了概念重叠？
如果有人坚持把单个 neuron 当成稳定语义单位，你会用这篇文章的哪条证据来反驳？
questions_en: Why is superposition better understood as a geometric outcome rather than merely a training bug?
In your toy-model sweep, which change most clearly altered concept overlap: hidden dimension, sparsity, or the loss term?
If someone insists on treating a single neuron as a stable semantic unit, which piece of evidence from this article would you use to push back?

questions_zh: 和直接看 neuron 相比，SAE 恢复出的 feature 到底多给了你什么可解释性优势？
在你的 sweep 里，monosemanticity 是从哪里开始变差的，你认为主因更像是容量、正则化，还是数据噪声？
如果恢复出来的 feature 不够稳定，你下一步会先改哪个变量家族，为什么？
questions_en: Compared with inspecting raw neurons directly, what interpretability advantage do SAE-style recovered features actually give you?
Where did monosemanticity start to degrade in your sweep, and do you think the main cause looked more like capacity, regularization, or data noise?
If the recovered features are not stable enough, which variable family would you change next, and why?

questions_zh: 为什么 feature catalog 会把 interpretability 从“挑几个酷例子”推进到“做地图”？
如果让你扩展这个教学版 catalog，你最想补哪一类 feature，为什么？
仅仅展示很多 feature 卡片，还不能支持哪些更强的研究结论？
questions_en: Why does a feature catalog move interpretability from 'collecting cool examples' toward 'making a map'?
If you had to extend this teaching catalog, which class of features would you add first, and why?
What stronger research claims are still unsupported even if you can show many feature cards?

questions_zh: 当 feature 能直接喂给 classifier 时，这说明了什么，又还没有说明什么？
如果 probe 准确率很高，你会先检查哪几个 confounders，再决定是否相信这个结果？
除了 accuracy，你还会用什么 baseline 或对照来判断这些 feature 真的有读出价值？
questions_en: If features can feed a classifier directly, what does that show, and what does it still not show?
If the probe accuracy is high, which confounders would you check before deciding the result is trustworthy?
Besides accuracy, which baseline or comparison would you use to judge whether the features really have readout value?

questions_zh: 你会怎么区分 target metric 和 off-target metric，它们为什么必须同时存在？
在你的 steering sweep 里，sweet spot 出现在哪一段，你认为为什么会在那里出现？
什么样的证据会让你决定放弃这条 steering 方向，而不是继续调强度？
questions_en: How would you distinguish the target metric from the off-target metric, and why do you need both?
Where did the sweet spot appear in your steering sweep, and why do you think it appeared there?
What evidence would make you abandon this steering direction rather than just tuning the strength further?

questions_zh: 不用照抄图上的标签，解释这张 attribution graph 里最重要的 3 条贡献路径在做什么。
这张图支持什么结论，又明确不支持什么结论？
如果你只能再做一个后续实验来降低这张图的歧义，你会选什么？
questions_en: Without simply reading back the labels, explain what the three most important contribution paths in this attribution graph are doing.
What conclusion does the graph support, and what conclusion does it clearly not support?
If you could run only one follow-up experiment to reduce the ambiguity in this graph, what would you choose?

questions_zh: 在 tracing workflow 里，哪一个步骤最像瓶颈，为什么？
如果你要给研究工程师提一个优先实现的工具需求，你会提什么，怎么衡量它值不值得做？
为什么说工具层不仅影响效率，还会改变团队能问什么研究问题？
questions_en: Which step in the tracing workflow looks most like the bottleneck, and why?
If you had to propose one tooling improvement to a research engineer, what would it be and how would you measure whether it is worth building?
Why does the tooling layer shape not just efficiency, but also the kinds of research questions a team can ask?

questions_zh: persona vectors 让哪些原本模糊的 character 属性变得可比较、可测量？
即使一个向量方向在当前 artifact 里有效，character control 仍然可能在哪些地方漂移或失稳？
如果你要评估 persona control 的稳定性，你会在不同 prompt、任务或时序上怎么测？
questions_en: Which previously fuzzy character properties become comparable or measurable once you have persona vectors?
Even if one vector direction works in the current artifact, where could character control still drift or destabilize?
If you had to evaluate the stability of persona control, how would you test it across prompts, tasks, or time?

questions_zh: 为什么 self-report 不能被直接当成内部状态的证据？
在这些 mismatch case 里，哪一个最值得继续追，替代解释可能有哪些？
如果你想把“内省迹象”这个 claim 说得更强，还缺哪类关键证据？
questions_en: Why can self-report not be treated as direct evidence of internal state?
Among these mismatch cases, which one is most worth following up, and what alternative explanations should you worry about?
If you wanted to make the 'signs of introspection' claim stronger, which key class of evidence is still missing?

questions_zh: 为什么用一条 assistant axis 来压缩多种风格是有用的，但又不够？
沿着这条轴，哪些属性看起来会平滑变化，哪些属性明显不会？
如果给你两周时间研究 character stabilization，你会围绕这条轴设计什么最小实验？
questions_en: Why is it useful to compress many assistant styles into one assistant axis, yet still insufficient?
Which properties seem to change smoothly along the axis, and which ones clearly do not?
If you had two weeks to study character stabilization, what smallest useful experiment would you design around this axis?
